item.title: Efficacy and Safety of Second-Line Advanced Therapy After Vedolizumab in Ulcerative Colitis: A Multicenter Cohort Study From the GETAID.
item.description: United European gastroenterology journal - Multicenter study - Vedolizumab has become the preferred first-line advanced therapy in ulcerative colitis (UC). However, the optimal second-line treatment following vedolizumab failure remains unclear. We aimed to ev...

item.title: Higher Colonic Tissue Drug Concentrations of Subcutaneous Compared to Intravenous Administration of Infliximab Therapy in Patients With Inflammatory Bowel Disease.
item.description: United European gastroenterology journal - Observational study - Intestinal tissue levels of infliximab (IFX) in patients with inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) treated with subcutaneous (SC) therapy have not been previously assessed. To compare serum and colonic...

item.title: Personalised treatment in biologically naive patients with Crohn's disease: a systematic review of the last 10 years.
item.description: Scandinavian journal of gastroenterology - Review - Biological treatment has revolutionised the management of Crohn's disease (CD), however, at baseline (pre-treatment), it is not possible to predict which biologic a patient will respond the best to...

item.title: Therapeutic drug monitoring in inflammatory bowel disease patients switching from intravenous to subcutaneous infliximab: insights from a prospective real-world study.
item.description: Scandinavian journal of gastroenterology - The efficacy and safety of switching from intravenous (IV) to subcutaneous (SC) infliximab (IFX) have been demonstrated across multiple studies, but data on patients treated with high IV doses rema...

item.title: Matrine improves bile acid metabolism and reduces inflammatory and oxidative stress in colitis via the JAK2 pathway.

item.title: Short and medium-term outcomes of ustekinumab biosimilar in patients with Crohn's disease in naïve patients and after switching from originator.

item.title: Lower drug clearance of adalimumab is associated with proactive therapeutic drug monitoring and mucosal healing in patients with inflammatory bowel disease.

item.title: Advancing drug discovery for Inflammatory bowel diseases through human intestinal organoid-based models.

item.title: Orally deliverable Perilla frutescens-derived nanovesicles as natural bioactive nanocarriers for colon-targeted colitis therapy via microenvironment reprogramming.

item.title: Viral encephalitis related to adalimumab in a 16-year-old Crohn's disease patient: case report and literature review.
